L1.area: Server-side message protocol parsing and request handling

L2.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the message size parsing logic. A remote attacker can send a crafted request with an extremely large decimal size value that causes integer overflow, bypassing payload size limits and crashing the server.

L3.area: Server utility functions for protocol message size parsing
L3.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the message size parsing logic. When parsing the size field from incoming PUB commands, an extremely large decimal string exceeding normal integer bounds causes integer overflow, allowing the parsed value to bypass maximum payload size checks and leading to a server crash.

ground_truth.reason: The parseSize function does not validate the length of the input decimal string, allowing an attacker to send a crafted request with an extremely large size value that overflows the integer, bypassing maxpayload checks and crashing the server.
